questions.q: Who won the Masters Tournament in 2026, marking his second consecutive victory?
questions.correctText: Rory McIlroy became the fourth player in Masters history to win back-to-back titles, following Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo, and Tiger Woods.

questions.q: Which Australian teenager set a new world U20 record in the 200m race with a time of 19.67 seconds at the 2026 Australian Athletics Championships?
questions.correctText: Gout Gout broke the world U20 record for the 200m with a time of 19.67 seconds, also setting a new Australian record and becoming faster than Usain Bolt at the same age.

questions.q: Doc Rivers is stepping down as head coach of which NBA team after a dismal season?
questions.correctText: Doc Rivers is departing as the head coach of the Milwaukee Bucks after the team finished with a 30-52 record.

questions.q: Which former major league infielder and manager, known as 'Scrap Iron,' died at the age of 76?
questions.correctText: Phil Garner, a three-time All-Star infielder and manager who led the Houston Astros to their first World Series appearance, has died at 76.

questions.q: Bob Hall, a pioneer in wheelchair racing and a two-time Boston Marathon champion, passed away at what age?
questions.correctText: Bob Hall, who was the first official wheelchair champion of the Boston Marathon in 1975 and also won in 1977, has died at the age of 74.

questions.q: During the third round of the 2026 Masters, Shane Lowry achieved a hole-in-one on which hole?
questions.correctText: Shane Lowry recorded a hole-in-one on the par-3 sixth hole during the third round of the 2026 Masters Tournament at Augusta National.

questions.q: Former Miami defensive end Rueben Bain Jr. was involved in a fatal car accident in which year?
questions.correctText: Rueben Bain Jr. was involved in a car accident in March 2024 that resulted in the death of a passenger three months later.
